vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but fails to account for the severe decline of P3 and the near-total loss of P2/P4. The white rabbit anchor remains in P3.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (Silver Guest - no viable foliage detected)

plant_audit.P1: Stable, foliage remains consistent in the yellow pot.
plant_audit.P2: Critical. No healthy mint foliage visible; soil surface shows no active growth.
plant_audit.P3: Critical. The Pothos shows significant chlorosis and necrosis. The large leaf is yellowing and drooping.
plant_audit.P4: Critical. No evidence of the Silver Guest specimen.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, granular, with visible white perlite/debris.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of electronic wiring/sensors in P3/P4 pots; white rabbit figurine is the only stable anchor.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual yellowing of the P3 Pothos leaf; P2/P4 remain static and devoid of healthy growth.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: P3 leaf necrosis has accelerated; the yellowing has deepened, indicating potential root rot or severe nutrient deficiency.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of rapid decline. P3 is suffering from severe physiological stress (likely overwatering or root suffocation given the yellowing). P2 and P4 are effectively lost.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit figurine in P3 and electronic sensor components in the soil of P3/P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting classic signs of terminal decline, with the primary leaf turning necrotic. The other specimens (P2/P4) are not visible as living plants, suggesting a systemic failure of the growing medium or environmental conditions. P1 remains the only stable element.
